text.title: Consumer Contract Tests Generated from OpenAPI / Traffic Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Author asks an LLM to write the Pact JSON inline. Contract drifts from the OpenAPI within two sprints.
antipattern.root-cause: No generator pipeline; LLM treated as a one-shot author.
antipattern.fix: Wire the generator (MCP/skill) into CI; reject hand-authored Pact files at PR time.

antipattern.symptom: Generator config reads `openapi.yaml` from the provider's main branch tip. can-i-deploy passes today, breaks tomorrow.
antipattern.root-cause: Source URI uses a branch ref, not a commit hash.
antipattern.fix: Force the generator to record the commit SHA in the Pact file metadata.

antipattern.symptom: Developer tweaks `pacts/*.json` to make a flaky test pass.
antipattern.root-cause: No read-only enforcement on generated outputs.
antipattern.fix: Add a pre-commit hook that rejects diffs in generated paths unless the commit message has `regen:` prefix.
